topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. A profile picture I hadn't seen in fifteen years: Liam. My childhood tormentor. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles strain beneath my skin. A stupid, misplaced impulse made me tap on the request, a slow, deliberate movement that felt heavy with resentment. I stared at the empty space where his name appeared in my list, my knuckles white where I gripped the phone. Every fiber of my being wanted to snatch the device and throw it against the wall, but I held still, a coiled spring vibrating with barely contained fury. *Why now?* the thought echoed in the hollow space where my composure used to reside.

[story 2]

I almost didn't answer. My thumb hovered over the 'decline' button, a temptation to simply erase the intrusion. But the sight of his name, bold and unyielding, kept my finger suspended. A hot flush crept up my neck, warming my cheeks. I took a deep, ragged breath, the air catching painfully in my chest. When I finally typed a message, the words were clipped, sharp edges honed by weeks of simmering frustration. "What do you want?" I reread it three times, the tone laced with something brittle and utterly dismissive. I watched the cursor blink, impatient, eager for this confrontation to end so I could move on to something less irritating.

[story 3]

The sound of the incoming request made me flinch, a sudden jerk of my whole frame. I slammed my laptop shut, the resulting thud vibrating through the desk. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small room like a caged animal. My hands clenched into fists at my sides, the tension radiating outward. Seeing his face, even as a digital image, brought a sour taste to my mouth. I didn't want to talk. I wanted to dismantle the request, erase the possibility of contact entirely. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I stared at the screen, willing the connection to simply vanish into the ether.
